title: romulea subpalustris
intro: Romulea subpalustris, also known as marsh romulea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to South Africa. The medicinal properties of Romulea subpalustris include its use as an anti-inflammatory agent, which is attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Romulea subpalustris is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal use, and it has also been used in traditional South African medicine for various purposes.
